title: Co-Designing Parkinson’s Care: Hybrid Open House Outcomes
keywords: hybrid open house with 7 interactive stations

summary.hook: This poster evaluates a Parkinson’s disease (PD) community education “open house” delivered in a hybrid format (in-person at ASU Tempe campus clinic and live via Zoom).
summary.summary_30s: This poster evaluates a Parkinson’s disease (PD) community education “open house” delivered in a hybrid format (in-person at ASU Tempe campus clinic and live via Zoom). The project aimed to describe how people with PD, care partners, and professionals perceived the event and to identify benefits, unmet needs, and priorities for future education initiatives. Survey responses before and after the event suggest increased familiarity with therapy options and where to find help—especially among people with PD and care partners—while professionals’ ratings remained stable. Qualitative comments emphasized appreciation for concrete information, social connection, and exposure to specific programs such as SPEAK OUT! and group offerings.
talktrack_2m: Focus: Describe perceptions of a PD-focused community education event among people living with PD, their caregivers, and professionals who work with PD.

Approach: Population: Three stakeholder groups: people with PD, care partners, and professionals who work with PD. Primary outcome: Perceptions/ratings related to knowledge and connection, including importance of addressing communication/swallowing/cognition, familiarity with therapy options, confidence in knowing where to find help, feeling connected to others, and interest in participating in therapy (items shown in figures).
Finding: Approximately 50 individuals attended the event.

Why it matters: Authors’ stated conclusions: The hybrid open house appeared to meet its primary goals (knowledge increase and connection to services), and findings support feasibility and perceived usefulness of community-based education for PD stakeholders. Implications (poster-grounded): Community-based education events may improve resource awareness and therapy familiarity for people with PD and care partners. Hybrid format can serve as a bridge between community members and healthcare professionals/providers. Mechanism/explanation (ONLY if stated): Not stated on poster (mechanism limited to education improving knowledge/confidence and psychosocial connection). Connections to prior work (ONLY if referenced): * A’Campo et al., 2010; Hellqvist et al., 2020; Gopala Krishna Pillai et al., 2023; Sepp et al., 2023 (caregiver education referenced in text though full citation shown for A’Campo and Hellqvist; Sepp referenced in narrative).
key_numbers: Pre-event survey respondents: 13 PD, 7 care partners, 5 professionals; post-event: 11 PD, 4 care partners, 3 professionals.
Qualitative comments emphasized appreciation for concrete information, social connection, and exposure to specific programs such as SPEAK OUT! and group offerings. * Objective(s...

takeaways: Pre-event survey responses: 13 people with PD, 7 care partners, 5 professionals; post-event survey responses: 11 people with PD, 4 care partners, 3 professionals.

Across stakeholder groups, the open house “appeared to meet its primary goals” of increasing knowledge about PD-related communication/swallowing/cognition resources and strengthening connections to services.
limitations: The poster does not list formal study limitations.
The poster leaves open this clarification: What were the exact survey items and the rating scale anchors (e.g., 1–5, 1–7)?

what_to_ask.question: In the patient chart (P5.F1), which specific items improved the most from pre to post (familiarity vs confidence vs connection)?
what_to_ask.why_this_is_good: This gets the presenter to interpret the main result instead of repeating the title.

what_to_ask.question: Were the pre- and post-event respondent samples overlapping, and how might nonresponse bias influence the trend interpretation?
what_to_ask.why_this_is_good: This probes confidence, assumptions, or limitations without sounding hostile.

what_to_ask.question: Based on your results, what station topics should be prioritized if you scale this open house to a larger event?
what_to_ask.why_this_is_good: This opens a useful conversation about why the work matters or where it goes next.
